sutta: the buddha said monks whatsoever monk or none disavows the training and returns to the lower life 5 matters for self-blame and reproach from the standpoint of dhamma come to him here and now what 5 he thinks in you there was verily no faith in skillful states kusala dhamma no conscientiousness no remorse no diligence and no wisdom into skillful states monks whatsoever monk or none disavows the training and returns to the lower life these 5 matters for self-blame and reproach from the standpoint of dhamma come to him here and now monks whatsoever among or none lives the holy life in perfect purity though with tearful face he cries in pain and distress 5 matters for self-praise from the standpoint of dhamma come to him here and now what 5 he thinks in you verily there was faith in skillful states conscientiousness remorse diligence and wisdom in skillful states monks these 5 matters for self-praise come to him here and now that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the buddha says if a monk or a nun disrobes and returns to the lower life meaning the life of a lay person then actually he did not cultivate i did not have the 5 learner's powers and because of that now he had this rope whereas another person even though wearing the ropes is painful and even tears come to his eyes and he cries in pain and distress yet if he still continues to wear the ropes then he can praise himself with these 5 learners powers that msc has these 5 learners powers now sometimes there are some people with very strong hindrances they find it very difficult to wear the rope what are these 5 hindrances 1 is sensual desire there's some people with very strong sensual desire and it always disturbs them another 1 is uh ill will a lot of anger when you have a lot of anger it's very difficult to live in a community of other monks or nuns then the third 1 is sloth and topper if you have a lot of sloth and topper it's very difficult to live the holy life it's always just you find it very difficult to make progress the fourth 1 is restlessness and worry restlessness is something that is very difficult for new monks because as a lay person when you feel restless you think oh i'm gonna do tonight i feel so restless i can't stay in this house all the 4 walls around me i feel like i have to climb up the wall so what do you do you go out and see a show they go to town and go to the beach you just go out and do something whereas as a monk you can't you have to stay in the monastery you're staying in a cave you just have to stay in your cave you just grit your teeth and bear it that's why the buddha said here sometimes even with a tearful face you still buried nah that's uh then you can praise yourself because this is a stage where monks and nuns have to go through if you can grit your teeth and bear it nah later on and you get used to it you master it then it doesn't disturb you later on however you find sometimes some monks they don't overcome the hindrances and even after 5 years they disrobe some after 10 years 20 years 30 years some even 40 years or so they still disrobe because the 5 hindrances disturb them and so uh the the only way to overcome the hindrances according to the buddha is to have cultivate samadhi contribute concentration 1 pointedness of mind and when you have 1 pointedness of mind the 5 hindrances are abandoned the other thing about these 5 learners powers the first 1 is having unshakable faith and as i said before you can only have it if you have a good understanding of the sutas the buddha's discourses that's why the buddha always calls his disciples savaakas and when he uses the word savaka's disciples he assumes that all of them are aryans that's why in the suttas the buddha always talks about the aryan disciple aryan disciple and so to me the the the sutas are so important that when we come into the dhamma into the the holy path and the spiritual partner we have to study the sutas and understand because from understanding then we have this unshakable faith and when you have unshakable faith and the buddha said now you have entered the stream you will never be reborn into the woeful plains again you're you're into the spiritual path you've entered the spiritual path without a proper understanding of the sutas you still have not entered the stream you're still outside the door you have not entered the door yet so that's why i always stress the importance of learning the sutas now we come to another sutra
